findings.description: The PDF presents the agenda as a free-form layout without table borders, while the HTML renders it as a formal table with visible row separators and column headers (Time, Topic, Presenter). The data content and structure are preserved, but the visual presentation differs from the original.
findings.evidence: PDF: Agenda items presented in a three-column layout without table borders or grid lines. HTML: Same content rendered inside a bordered table with header row and alternating row styling.

findings.description: The PDF does not present the agenda as a bordered table with column headers. The HTML introduces a structured table with 'Time', 'Topic', and 'Presenter' column headers that do not exist in the source document. However, the underlying data is accurate.
findings.evidence: HTML adds <thead> with <th>Time</th>, <th>Topic</th>, <th>Presenter</th> — these headers are not in the PDF. The PDF uses a simple layout without explicit table headers.

findings.description: The HTML contains nested <main> elements, resulting in a duplicated structural wrapper. While the content itself is not repeated, this is a structural duplication issue.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> containing another <main> — two nested main elements.
